subject: [SDC] Add chart for helm-validator microservice
commit_message:
[SDC] Add chart for helm-validator microservice

Add microservice for SDC that allows to validate Helm
charts package with various versions of Helm

Issue-ID: SDC-3185
Signed-off-by: Remigiusz Janeczek <remigiusz.janeczek@nokia.com>
Change-Id: Ic9c4305175d6418d472ac6554b661bfde89ed17d

Patch Set 4:

This change is ready for review.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(2 comments)

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

Well, the Helm versions present in this container depend on MultiCloud/K8S plugin.
When we validate the CNF Helm chart, we validate with the same Helm client version as in the MultiCloud/K8S, which is still Helm v2... 
Validating against a different version makes little-to-no sense...

Asked Lukasz Rajewski to let us know, what are the plans in MultiCloud/K8S plugin.

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

If multi-cloud plugin still uses helm libs in version 2.x we should be this version in the helm validation docker image.
Btw. probably there are VNF packages in use there are in version v1 meant to be run with helm 2.x.

In my opinion we should remove the 2.x version from the image if multi-cloud will be switched to version 3.x.

We should also announce that from now on VNF packages should be created in version v2 (helm 3.x).

Patch Set 4:

(2 comments)

Some comments

Patch Set 4:

> Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1
> 
> If multi-cloud plugin still uses helm libs in version 2.x we should be this version in the helm validation docker image.
> Btw. probably there are VNF packages in use there are in version v1 meant to be run with helm 2.x.
> 
> In my opinion we should remove the 2.x version from the image if multi-cloud will be switched to version 3.x.
> 
> We should also announce that from now on VNF packages should be created in version v2 (helm 3.x).

Well, Helm 3.5 still supports the v2 version of the helm package but in the release notes of multicloud it will be mentioned for sure - once released.

Patch Set 5:

@Krzysztof - moreover you are not the one who decides about the inner content of the components (like SDC). You job is to review if the code (helm charts) and ensure that it is in good quality and in accordance to conventions set up in OOM sub-project (and only that!).

Patch Set 5: Code-Review+1

Dears - let`s cool down a little.
There is already a PS5, which refers to an image, which does not contain Helm v2.

The next step to to validate that image in gating, if that one works well, we`ll merge it.
The change will be introduced in Istanbul, and we expect MultiCloud-K8S to update to Helm v3 in this timeframe. 

If MultiCloud-K8S doesn`t update in Istanbul time-frame, then well - then we go back to the discussion, if ONAP is one project, or a set of loosely coupled projects.

Adding +1 on PS5.

@Damian, patch for helm3 support in k8splugin is ready and is waiting for proper moment to be merged so it will be for sure in the Istanbul release.

Patch Set 5:

> Patch Set 5:
> 
> @Damian, patch for helm3 support in k8splugin is ready and is waiting for proper moment to be merged so it will be for sure in the Istanbul release.

@Lukasz: OK, can we merge it already now? If we have a branch already in MultiCloud-K8S, it should be the right time to merge it (and then we are aligned between SDC and MultiCloud-K8S).

Are there any specific activities You`re waiting for in context of that MultiCloud-K8S?

Patch Set 5:

@Damian, as long as it does not break master and basic_cnf use case, you can.
